message:
fix(combinator/timeout): saturate Duration::MAX to Time::MAX in Timeout::after

Timeout::after previously cast duration.as_nanos() (u128) directly to
u64, silently truncating Duration::MAX to a small value.  This caused
"infinite" timeouts to fire almost immediately instead of never.

Add duration_to_nanos() helper (same pattern as transport/stream.rs)
that clamps to u64::MAX before the cast, so Duration::MAX produces
Time::MAX (the farthest representable deadline).

- New test: timeout_after_duration_saturates_large_duration verifying
  Timeout::after(now, Duration::MAX).deadline == Time::MAX

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
